recipes.title: Crepes
instructions: Mix all ingredients in a blender until smooth.

Cook in an 8–9 inch nonstick skillet over medium heat, no cooking spray needed. Pour a thin layer of batter and swirl to coat the pan. Cook until edges begin to set, then flip and cook briefly on the other side.

recipes.title: German Pancakes
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°. Melt butter in a 9x9 pan in the hot oven.
Beat together eggs, milk, flour, and salt. Pour into the hot buttered pan.
Bake 18 minutes until puffed and golden brown.

recipes.title: Maple Syrup
instructions: Follow the directions on the Mapleine Maple Extract bottle to make homemade maple syrup.

recipes.title: Pumpkin Pancakes with Buttermilk Syrup
instructions: For pancakes: Whisk together milk, pumpkin, oil, and eggs. Add flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt and stir until just combined. Cook on a greased griddle or skillet over medium heat.

For syrup: Combine butter, sugar, buttermilk, and corn syrup in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, stirring frequently. Remove from heat and stir in vanilla extract and baking soda — it will foam up. Serve warm over pancakes.

recipes.title: Alfredo Sauce

items: Minced garlic or garlic salt, to taste

instructions: Over medium heat, bring cream and butter to a boil. Remove from heat, add Parmesan cheese, and stir until melted.
Season with salt, pepper, and garlic. Serve over 16 oz cooked pasta.

recipes.title: Baked Fried Chicken
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°. Place chicken strips in a bowl and cover with milk; let soak 20–30 minutes.
Mix dry ingredients (salt, seasoned salt, pepper, flour, paprika) in a zip-lock bag. Cut butter into pieces and place in a 9x13 pan. Put pan in oven until butter is melted.
Shake excess milk off chicken and coat each piece with the dry mix. Place in pan with melted butter. Bake 40 minutes, turning halfway through.

recipes.title: Baked Ham Sandwiches
instructions: Place the bottoms of the rolls in a baking dish. Top with ham and cheese, cutting to size if necessary.

Melt butter and stir in Worcestershire sauce, garlic powder, onion powder, and poppy seeds. Brush the butter mixture over the sandwiches, using it all. Place tops on sandwiches and cover with foil. Chill several hours or overnight.
Bake at 375° for 15 minutes, removing the foil for the last few minutes to brown the tops.

recipes.title: Baked Macaroni & Cheese
instructions: Heat oven to 375°. Grease a 2-qt baking dish. Cook macaroni according to package directions; drain and rinse with cold water.
Melt ~1 Tbsp butter over low heat and mix in flour. Cook and stir about 1 minute, then gradually stir in milk. Add salt & pepper and bring to a simmer. Simmer 3 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add cheddar cubes and stir until partially melted. Remove from heat and stir in macaroni and Parmesan. Pour into baking dish. Top with buttered bread crumbs mixed with a little Parmesan.
Bake 25–30 minutes until crumbs are browned and mac is bubbly at edges.

recipes.title: Baked Parmesan Crusted Chicken
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°. Melt butter with garlic in a saucepan. Remove from heat and stir in bread crumbs, Parmesan, and herbs.
Dip each piece of chicken in the garlic butter, then press into the bread crumb mixture. Place in a greased 9x13 baking dish.
Bake 50 minutes until golden and cooked through.

recipes.title: Balsamic Chicken Sandwiches

items: 6 sub buns, ciabatta rolls, or 2 baguettes

instructions: Marinate chicken in balsamic vinaigrette overnight. Grill chicken until cooked through, then slice into thin strips.
If using baguettes, cut each into 3 pieces then slice open. Spread one side of each piece with 1 Tbsp pesto. Layer chicken, mozzarella, and tomato slices. Top with the other side of bread.
Serve immediately, or wrap in foil and heat 5–10 minutes in a 300° oven.

recipes.title: Beef Fajitas
instructions: In a skillet over medium-high heat, cook beef and onion in 2 Tbsp oil until meat is browned. Add bell peppers and cook several more minutes until crisp-tender.
Add tomatoes and taco seasoning combined with 1/4 c water. Cook just until water is gone. Serve on tortillas topped with sour cream, salsa, and cheese.

recipes.title: Beef Stroganoff
instructions: Cook pasta according to package directions. Meanwhile, heat 1 Tbsp oil in a large skillet and cook beef until no longer pink.

In a bowl, combine cream soup, sour cream, milk, and Worcestershire sauce; whisk until smooth. Add to the meat along with bouillon cubes. Cook over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, until warm and bubbly and bouillon is dissolved.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve sauce over drained pasta.

recipes.title: Browned Butter with Mizithra Cheese Pasta
instructions: Cook spaghetti according to package directions until tender. While pasta cooks, brown the butter in a saucepan over medium heat — it will foam then turn golden brown.
Drain pasta but do not rinse. Pour brown butter over pasta, sprinkle with mizithra cheese, and toss to coat. Garnish with parsley if desired.

recipes.title: Butternut Squash Risotto

items: 1 medium butternut squash, peeled and cubed

instructions: Heat the broth: Pour 6 cups broth into a medium pot over medium heat. Bring to a low simmer, then reduce heat and cover to keep warm.
Prep the squash: Peel, seed, and cut the butternut squash into 3/4-inch cubes.

Sauté the onion and squash: Heat olive oil in a large deep skillet over medium-low. Cook onion until soft and golden, about 8 minutes. Add squash and stir to coat with oil. Cook about 10 minutes until beginning to soften. Season with salt, pepper, rosemary, and sage. Cook until just tender, about 4 more minutes. Scoop out half the squash pieces and set aside.

Add the rice: Stir in Arborio rice and toast it, stirring 3–5 minutes until pearly and translucent. Ladle in 3/4–1 cup of hot broth, stirring constantly until fully absorbed. Continue adding broth by the ladleful, stirring regularly, until rice is creamy and al dente (about 20 minutes total).
Finish: Stir in the reserved squash pieces. Add a final splash of broth for a flowing, wavy consistency. Remove from heat and stir in Parmesan and butter until fully melted. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Calzone

items: Dough for 1 loaf of bread (store-bought or homemade)
Meat of choice: pepperoni, sausage, ham, or ground beef

instructions: Preheat oven to 375°. Divide dough into 4 pieces. Roll each piece into a circle about 10 inches in diameter.
Fill each circle with meat, cheese, and spaghetti sauce. Fold in half and pinch edges closed. Place on a greased baking sheet.
Bake about 20 minutes or until golden brown.

recipes.title: Chicken Alfredo
instructions: Heat cream cheese and milk over medium heat to a boil, stirring constantly. Stir in 1/2 c Parmesan. Reduce heat to low and cook 5 minutes.
Add chicken, peas, and pasta to the pot. Toss to combine, top with remaining Parmesan, and serve.
notes: For a more traditional sauce, substitute 2 Tbsp butter and 1 1/2 c heavy cream for the cream cheese and milk.

recipes.title: Chicken Burritos

items: 1/2 cup enchilada sauce or hot sauce (adjust to taste)

instructions: Sauté the diced pepper and onion in a large pan until just before they caramelize. Add the chicken and cook thoroughly.
Add cream cheese, enchilada sauce, corn, and beans. Stir until well combined and cream cheese is melted.
Fill tortillas with the chicken mixture and roll up. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Chicken Enchiladas

items: 1 can black or kidney beans, drained

instructions: Preheat oven to 350°. Grease a 9x13 baking dish.
In a bowl, combine cream of chicken soup, sour cream, reserved chicken broth, and enough milk to make a creamy sauce. In another bowl, break chicken into pieces and stir in beans.

Divide the chicken-bean mixture among the 12 tortillas. Top each with 1/4 c cheese and 2 Tbsp sauce. Roll up and place side by side in the baking dish. Pour remaining sauce over the top, covering all tortillas. Top with remaining cheese.
Bake 20–30 minutes until heated through. Serve with chopped olives, salsa, and guacamole.

recipes.title: Chicken Pillows

items: Garlic salt, salt & pepper to taste

instructions: Combine chicken, cream cheese, chives, and seasonings; stir until just mixed.
Unroll crescent rolls, pressing 2 together to form rectangles. Place 1/4 of filling on each rectangle, fold over, and pinch edges to seal.
Dip each pillow in melted butter, then roll in bread crumbs. Bake at 375° for 20 minutes or until golden brown. Serve topped with prepared chicken gravy.

recipes.title: Fish Tacos
instructions: Cook fish fillets according to package directions.
Mix together cabbage, sour cream, cilantro, lime juice, cumin, salt, and pepper to make the slaw.
Break or shred the cooked fish. Top each tortilla with fish, slaw, cheese, and salsa.

recipes.title: Ham Paninis
instructions: Heat a panini press or Foreman grill. For each sandwich, spread mayo on the inside of the bread, then layer ham, avocado, and cheese. Top with a second slice of bread and butter the outsides of both pieces.
Cook on the press or grill for 5 minutes, or until cheese is melted and outside is golden.

recipes.title: Hawaiian Haystack

ingredientGroups.heading: Base
items: 1 can chicken, or 2 chicken breasts cooked & shredded
1 can of milk (use empty soup can to measure)

instructions: Combine cream of chicken soup, milk, and chicken in a saucepan. Heat over medium heat until warm and bubbly.
Serve over rice with desired toppings.

recipes.title: Indian Butter Chicken

ingredientGroups.heading: Marinade
items: 1.5 lbs chicken breasts, cut into 1-inch cubes

instructions: Combine marinade ingredients in a large zip-lock bag. Add chicken cubes and let chill for at least 1 hour.
Add the chicken along with the marinade to a large pot over medium-high heat. Cook about 5 minutes. Add butter and garlic and cook until butter is melted.
Add tomato sauce, cream, and spices. Stir to combine, then simmer 20–30 minutes, stirring occasionally. Serve with rice and naan bread.

recipes.title: Marinated Grilled Chicken (Xango Chicken)

items: 2–3 lbs boneless skinless chicken tenders (thighs taste best)
1 tsp ground ginger (or shredded fresh ginger)

instructions: Mix all ingredients together and let chicken marinate for up to 24 hours.
Grill on an indoor or outdoor grill until no longer pink. Serves 4–6.

recipes.title: Orange Chicken
instructions: Bake chicken tenders according to package directions.
Combine all sauce ingredients in a saucepan. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until sauce is thick and bubbly.
Serve chicken and sauce over rice.

recipes.title: Philly Cheesesteak Sloppy Joe's
instructions: In a large cast iron skillet, flatten ground beef and cook over medium heat without stirring until the bottom is brown and crispy. Flip and continue cooking, breaking up the meat, until no pink remains. Remove meat from pan.
In the meat drippings, cook the onion, bell pepper, and mushrooms a few minutes until soft. Dissolve cornstarch in broth, then add ketchup, Worcestershire, salt, and pepper.

Return meat to pan and add the broth mixture. Simmer over low heat 5 minutes or until little liquid remains. Top toasted buns with meat and a slice of provolone. Broil briefly to melt cheese if desired.

recipes.title: Queso Mac & Cheese
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°. Dice the bell peppers and onion, spread on a greased baking sheet, and roast 30–40 minutes.
Combine roasted vegetables with chicken, enchilada sauce, green chiles, fire-roasted tomatoes, garlic, onion powder, salt, pepper, and cayenne in a slow cooker. Cook on low 3–4 hours.
Cook shell pasta for half the time shown on the box. Blend together the queso sauce ingredients (cottage cheese, cream cheese, cheddar, enchilada sauce, milk) until smooth.
Add the par-cooked pasta and blended queso sauce to the slow cooker with the chicken and vegetables. Cook on high 15–20 minutes until pasta is tender.

recipes.title: Quiche
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°. Cook onions and ham in butter until softened.
Spread grated cheese in the pastry shell. Add flour, ham, and onions and stir slightly. Pour beaten eggs over the mixture.
Bake 40 minutes until set.

recipes.title: Apple Brickle Dip
instructions: Beat cream cheese, sugars, and vanilla until smooth. Fold in toffee brickle chips.
Serve with apple slices. Refrigerate any leftovers.

recipes.title: Frog-Eye Salad

items: 8 oz acini de pepe pasta, cooked and drained
1 (10 oz) can pineapple tidbits, drained (reserve 1/4 c juice)

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the reserved pineapple juice, milk, sugar, and pudding mix for 2 minutes.
Gently fold in pasta, pineapple tidbits, crushed pineapple, mandarin oranges, marshmallows, coconut, and Cool Whip. Cover and chill several hours before serving.

recipes.title: Fruit Salad
instructions: Combine all fruit in a large bowl. Add vanilla yogurt and gently toss to coat. Serve immediately or refrigerate until ready to serve.

recipes.title: Spanish Rice
instructions: Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add rice and onion. Sauté, stirring constantly, until rice begins to brown.
Add water, tomato sauce, chicken bouillon, and garlic salt. Stir until blended and bring to a boil. Lay cilantro stems on top and reduce heat.
Cover and simmer 20 minutes or until all water is absorbed and rice is tender. Remove cooked cilantro and serve.

recipes.title: Spinach Salad

ingredientGroups.heading: Salad
items: 4–5 strips bacon, cooked crisp and crumbled

instructions: Combine spinach, celery, bacon, eggs, and mandarin oranges in a large bowl.
Whisk together all dressing ingredients. Toss with salad just before serving.

recipes.title: Turkish Flatbread (Naan Bread)
instructions: Combine yeast, sugar, and warm water in a large bowl and stir well. Allow to sit 5–10 minutes until foamy. Whisk in the Greek yogurt, olive oil, and salt. Add flour and stir until dough comes together. Knead 3–4 minutes. Divide into 20 equal pieces and cover with a kitchen towel. Rest 15 minutes.

Preheat a skillet to medium-low. Roll each piece into a 7-inch circle. Lightly brush the top with olive oil, then place oiled side down on the hot pan. Brush the top with oil. Cook 1–2 minutes until the surface is covered with bubbles and the underside is golden.
Flip and cook until the other side has golden brown spots. Place on a kitchen-towel-covered plate, sprinkle with parsley, and cover to keep moist.

recipes.title: French Silk Pie

items: 3 oz unsweetened chocolate, melted and cooled
Whipped cream and chocolate curls to garnish (optional)

instructions: Beat sugar and butter with an electric mixer on medium speed about 4 minutes until fluffy. Stir in melted chocolate and vanilla.
Add eggs one at a time, beating on high speed after each addition and scraping sides of bowl constantly.
Transfer to baked pastry shell. Cover and chill 5–24 hours until set. Top with whipped cream and chocolate curls before serving.

recipes.title: Fresh Peach Pie

items: 8 large fresh peaches, peeled and sliced

instructions: In a small saucepan, mix sugar, water, and cornstarch until dissolved. Heat over medium heat, stirring constantly, until mixture boils and thickens. Remove from heat and cool completely — glaze will thicken more as it cools.
Place peeled, sliced peaches in a large bowl. Pour half the glaze over the fruit and gently mix to coat. Add more glaze as needed.
Pour glazed peaches into the pie crust, piling as high as desired. Refrigerate several hours or overnight. Serve cold with whipped cream.

recipes.title: Ginger Snaps
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°. Cream butter and sugar until fluffy. Add egg and molasses and mix well. Stir in dry ingredients (flour, ginger, cinnamon, salt, baking soda).
Form into small balls and roll in sugar. Place on baking sheet and bake 10 minutes. Cool on a rack.

recipes.title: Hot Fudge Sauce
instructions: Melt butter and baking chocolate together in a saucepan. Add evaporated milk, powdered sugar, and vanilla extract.
Boil, stirring constantly, for 6 minutes or until sauce begins to thicken. Serve warm over ice cream.

recipes.title: Lemon Bars
instructions: Mix melted butter with sugar, vanilla, and salt. Add flour and blend — dough will be thick like cookie dough. Press into the bottom of a 9x9 glass baking pan. Bake at 350° for 20 minutes or until edges begin to brown.

Whisk eggs, sugar, and flour until thick and light yellow. Slowly add lemon juice, whisking as you pour. Pour lemon filling over hot crust and return to oven. Bake about 25 minutes until completely set.
Remove from oven and cool completely. Refrigerate at least 2 hours until chilled. Dust with powdered sugar before serving.

recipes.title: Peanut Butter Fudge
instructions: Combine butter and peanut butter in a saucepan. Cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until melted. Add vanilla and powdered sugar and stir to combine.

Spread into a buttered 8x8 pan lined with parchment paper. Fold excess parchment over the surface and refrigerate until firm. Cut into 1-inch pieces. Store at room temperature in an airtight container up to 1 week.

recipes.title: Pretzel Jello Salad
instructions: Mix crushed pretzels, melted butter, and sugar. Press into the bottom of a 9x13 baking dish. Bake at 350° for 8–10 minutes. Let cool.
Beat cream cheese, sugar, and Cool Whip until smooth. Spread over the cooled crust, making sure to cover all the pretzels and seal the edges completely.
Dissolve Jello in 2 c boiling water. Stir in frozen strawberries until combined. Pour over the cream cheese layer and chill until completely set.

recipes.title: Shannon's Raspberry Cake

items: 1 white cake mix (plus ingredients as directed)

instructions: Bake white cake on a sheet cake pan for 20 minutes. Cool completely.

Thaw raspberries and drain juice. Add enough water to the juice to equal 1 1/3 cups total liquid. Combine with raspberry Danish Dessert and microwave, stirring until thick (3–5 minutes). Stir in raspberries and let cool.
Beat powdered sugar with softened cream cheese until smooth. Fold in Cool Whip.
Spread cream cheese mixture over cooled cake. Spread raspberry topping over the cream cheese layer. Chill and serve.

recipes.title: Pesto
instructions: Combine basil, Parmesan, and garlic in a food processor and pulse until puréed.
With the processor running, add olive oil slowly until well combined. Freeze leftovers in an ice cube tray for easy portioning.

recipes.title: Cafe Rio Cilantro Lime Ranch

items: Buttermilk and mayonnaise (as directed on package)

instructions: Prepare ranch dressing according to package directions. Add cilantro, Tabasco, and a squeeze of lime juice.
Blend or process until smooth and cilantro is fully incorporated. Refrigerate until ready to serve.

recipes.title: Garam Masala
instructions: Mix all spices together in a small bowl. Transfer to an airtight container and store in a cool, dry place.

recipes.title: Creamy Tomato Rosa Chicken Pasta
instructions: Boil Pasta: Cook pasta in salted water. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining.
Sear Chicken: Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high. Season chicken with salt, pepper, garlic powder, and half the Italian seasoning. Sauté until cooked through; remove and set aside.
Sauté Veggies: In the same skillet, cook stir-fry vegetables until tender-crisp.
Simmer Sauce: Lower heat. Add tomato sauce, cream cheese, honey, and remaining Italian seasoning. Stir until cream cheese is fully melted and sauce is pink.
Combine: Fold in the cooked pasta and chicken. Add reserved pasta water in small splashes until the sauce is glossy and coats everything evenly.
Finish: Stir in Parmesan cheese. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Topgolf Queso French Fries

ingredientGroups.heading: Queso
items: 6 oz Monterey Jack cheese, block -- shred yourself for best melt
4 oz Pepper Jack cheese, block -- shred yourself
1/4 cup milk (add more to thin if needed)
1 can (10 oz) Rotel diced tomatoes & green chiles, drained

ingredientGroups.heading: Toppings
items: Bacon bits or cooked, crumbled bacon strips

instructions: Bake the fries. Cook crinkle cut fries according to package directions (typically 425°F for 25-30 min). Season with salt right when they come out.
Sauté the onion. Melt butter in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Add onion and cook until soft and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Reduce heat to medium-low.
Melt in the cheese. Add cream cheese first and stir until melted. Then add the Monterey Jack and Pepper Jack in handfuls, stirring constantly until smooth. Add milk to loosen if needed.
Finish the queso. Stir in the drained Rotel and garlic powder. Season with salt. Keep warm on low heat, stirring occasionally.
Assemble. Pile fries onto a plate or oven-safe dish. Pour queso generously over the top. Add bacon, jalapeños, green onions, and a dollop of sour cream.
Serve immediately while fries are hot and crispy.
notes: Shred cheese yourself from blocks -- pre-shredded cheese contains anti-caking agents that prevent it from melting smoothly.
To keep the queso warm for serving, transfer to a small slow cooker on the "warm" setting.

recipes.title: Chickpea Salad

ingredientGroups.heading: Dressing
items: 2 tablespoons minced fresh dill, plus more for serving
2 tablespoons minced fresh parsley, plus more for serving

ingredientGroups.heading: Salad
items: 1/2 cup thinly sliced scallions, white and green parts (2 to 3 scallions)

instructions: In a small bowl, combine the yogurt, mayonnaise, lemon juice, mustard, salt and pepper. Whisk until smooth, then add the dill and parsley and stir to combine. Set aside.
Place the chickpeas in a large bowl and using a fork, lightly mash about 1/3 of them. Add the celery and scallions and toss.

Pour the dressing over the salad, toss well, and set aside at room temperature for at least 30 minutes before serving. Sprinkle with more dill and parsley and serve. (If you're not serving the dish immediately, you can store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 days. Let sit at room temperature for 30 minutes before serving.)

recipes.title: Lemon-Lime Delight Bars

ingredientGroups.heading: Filling
items: 2 Tbsp. fresh lime juice & zest to taste

instructions: Combine butter, powdered sugar, and flour, mixing well. Press into 9×13 baking pan and bake 15 minutes at 350 degrees.
Blend eggs, sugar, flour, juices, and zest together; pour into slightly cooled crust. Bake at 350 degrees for 25 minutes or until set.
Chill and sprinkle with additional powdered sugar if desired.
notes: Throw a few of these bars into a blender with some vanilla ice cream. You'll have a refreshing shake with cookie bits!
